title: The One With Joey's Big Break

jokes.text: Richard? I'm not gonna go see your ex-boyfriend.
jokes.setup: Monica suggests Rachel see her eye doctor
jokes.punchline: Rachel assumes it's Monica's ex-boyfriend Richard
jokes.explanation: Character-driven humor based on Rachel's immediate assumption about Monica's dating history. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: Richard, that's all I ever hear. Richard, Richard, Richard.
jokes.setup: Rachel complaining about hearing Richard's name
jokes.punchline: The repetition emphasizes her annoyance
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation through repetition, showing Rachel's frustration. Basic but effective.

jokes.text: Since we've been going out, I think I've mentioned his name twice. Okay. So Richard, Richard.
jokes.setup: Monica defends herself saying she rarely mentions Richard
jokes.punchline: She immediately mentions his name twice more while defending herself
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic moment where Monica proves Rachel's point while trying to disprove it. Good character writing.

jokes.text: Any time anything comes close to touching her eye or anyone else's... she, like, freaks out. Watch.
jokes.setup: Monica explains Rachel's eye phobia
jokes.punchline: The demonstration setup creates anticipation
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag about Rachel's eye phobia. More setup than punchline.

jokes.text: Me, myself and eye.
jokes.setup: Rachel has established her eye phobia
jokes.punchline: Chandler replaces 'I' with 'eye' in the song title
jokes.explanation: Simple pun on De La Soul song. Economical and fits Chandler's wordplay style.

jokes.text: All those in favor, say 'eye.'
jokes.setup: Continuing the eye pun attack on Rachel
jokes.punchline: Replacing 'aye' with 'eye' in voting phrase
jokes.explanation: Second eye pun in the series. Less original but maintains the running gag momentum.

jokes.text: How much did I love The King and 'Eye.'
jokes.setup: Third eye pun in succession
jokes.punchline: King and I becomes King and Eye
jokes.explanation: Third pun shows diminishing returns but completes the assault on Rachel's phobia.

jokes.text: You okay there, man? Yeah. I got too excited.
jokes.setup: Chandler groans after the eye pun session
jokes.punchline: He admits he got too carried away with the wordplay
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chandler's self-awareness about his pun addiction.

jokes.text: Why are you so mad at him? Look, I don't wanna talk about it, okay? Well, it seems like... You wanna be on my list too? Keep talking.
jokes.setup: Chandler tries to mediate Rachel's anger at Ross
jokes.punchline: Rachel threatens to add Chandler to her enemies list
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Rachel's vindictive side and threatening innocent bystanders.

jokes.text: Has anyone seen my list, by the way?
jokes.setup: Rachel just mentioned her list of people she's mad at
jokes.punchline: She's lost the physical list
jokes.explanation: Absurd detail that she has an actual physical enemies list that she's misplaced.

jokes.text: Uh, no. What's it look like? Um, a piece of paper and it says 'Ross.' I see.
jokes.setup: Chandler asks what the list looks like
jokes.punchline: It's just Ross's name on paper, showing the list's simplicity and focus
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail - the 'list' is just Ross's name, showing her single-minded focus on her anger.

jokes.text: Yeah, um, I meet this girl in the subway and we fall in love in, like, a day, right? And then she disappears. But I find out where she lives. And when I get there, this old lady answers the door. I say, 'Where's Betsy?' Right? And she says, 'Betsy's been dead 10 years.'
jokes.setup: Joey excitedly describes his movie's plot
jokes.punchline: The clichéd supernatural romance twist ending
jokes.explanation: Sets up the movie's terrible plot that becomes a running joke. Joey's enthusiasm for obvious schlock.

jokes.text: Ooh. Chilling.
jokes.setup: Joey's dramatic movie plot revelation
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic understated response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler response - economical sarcasm that deflates Joey's excitement.

jokes.text: Oh, wait. My grandmother's dead.
jokes.setup: Joey and Chandler planning road trip logistics
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's non-sequitur announcement
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe misdirection - delivers death news as casual aside. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Well, uh, we can talk about that too, Pheebs.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's casual death announcement
jokes.punchline: Chandler's awkward attempt to be supportive
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Chandler's discomfort with emotional topics.

jokes.text: No, no, her cab. She probably won't be using it.
jokes.setup: Everyone thinks Phoebe wants to discuss grandmother's death
jokes.punchline: She's just offering practical transportation solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - death as practical opportunity. Dark humor with character-specific delivery.

jokes.text: Whoa, whoa, whoa. What are we gonna do about my job? Oh, uh, not go. All right, great. Road trip, baby.
jokes.setup: Chandler worries about work responsibilities
jokes.punchline: Joey's instant dismissal and Chandler's immediate acceptance
jokes.explanation: Shows Joey's carefree attitude overriding Chandler's brief moment of responsibility.

jokes.text: Look what I did. I mean, look at this mess. I mean, we're gonna probably have to clean this up, you know? I mean, we're gonna have to reschedule.
jokes.setup: Rachel at eye doctor appointment
jokes.punchline: She's making desperate excuses to avoid the appointment
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor from Rachel's transparent avoidance tactics. Good character work.

jokes.text: If you thought that this mess was gonna bother me, you are wrong. All right, let's go, Blinky.
jokes.setup: Rachel's attempt to derail the appointment
jokes.punchline: Monica's determination plus 'Blinky' nickname
jokes.explanation: Monica's controlling nature shines through with the condescending nickname.

jokes.text: Ooh, if you take the north route, there's a man in Illinois with a beard of bees.
jokes.setup: Joey asking for travel advice
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's bizarre roadside attraction knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Phoebe detail. 'Beard of bees' is memorably specific and weird.

jokes.text: But on the southern route, there's a chicken that plays tic-tac-toe.
jokes.setup: Following the beard of bees
jokes.punchline: Another equally absurd roadside attraction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurdity, though slightly less original than beard of bees.

jokes.text: Well, back to square one.
jokes.setup: Phoebe gives two equally compelling bizarre options
jokes.punchline: Joey treats this as a serious decision-making crisis
jokes.explanation: Joey's earnest treatment of absurd choices is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Rather sleep with Monica or Rachel? Monica. Oh. I always thought it'd be Rachel.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's rapid-fire question game
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate question gets honest answer, surprising Phoebe
jokes.explanation: Awkward sexual humor that plays on the game's premise of honest quick answers.

jokes.text: Beard of bees here I come.
jokes.setup: Decision-making game concludes
jokes.punchline: Joey's enthusiasm for the absurd destination
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Joey's commitment to seeing absurd roadside attractions.

jokes.text: Who would you rather sleep with, Monica or Rachel?
jokes.setup: Ross tries to replicate Phoebe's game
jokes.punchline: He asks the same inappropriate question
jokes.explanation: Weak callback that lacks the spontaneity of the original moment.

jokes.text: Dude, you are sick.
jokes.setup: Ross asks the inappropriate question about his sister and ex
jokes.punchline: Chandler's disgusted reaction
jokes.explanation: Appropriate disgusted reaction to Ross's inappropriate question about his sister.

jokes.text: Oh. I'm sorry. I forgot you had that whole Rachel thing.
jokes.setup: Chandler's disgusted reaction
jokes.punchline: Ross's oblivious misunderstanding of why it's inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment where Ross misses the real issue (Monica is his sister).

jokes.text: Uh, that's an eye removal machine. All right, I'm out of here.
jokes.setup: Rachel asks about scary-looking eye equipment
jokes.punchline: Doctor's joke backfires completely
jokes.explanation: Doctor's poor attempt at humor with someone who has eye phobia creates cringe.

jokes.text: Extended eye test sequence with Rachel repeatedly fleeing
jokes.setup: Simple eye test with air puff
jokes.punchline: Rachel's extreme physical comedy reactions and repeated failures
jokes.explanation: Extended physical comedy sequence built on established character trait.

jokes.text: You're young. You probably don't have glaucoma.
jokes.setup: Doctor's failed attempts to test Rachel
jokes.punchline: He gives up with medical non-diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Doctor's professional defeat delivered with medical authority.

jokes.text: I guess we'll see you back here in three months. Great. And I'll fit you for a glass eye.
jokes.setup: Doctor suggests return visit
jokes.punchline: Threatens glass eye to force compliance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and misdirection - doctor uses scare tactics effectively.

jokes.text: P-E-C-F-D. Very good, Monica.
jokes.setup: Monica apparently takes eye test
jokes.punchline: She's reading the chart perfectly while Rachel can't
jokes.explanation: Visual gag contrasting Monica's competence with Rachel's chaos.

jokes.text: And you don't get one.
jokes.setup: Monica gets something from the doctor
jokes.punchline: She denies Rachel whatever reward she received
jokes.explanation: Monica's petty competitiveness on display.

jokes.text: Those eye drops are a miracle. My eye is 100 percent better. They're still in my coat.
jokes.setup: Rachel praises the eye drops
jokes.punchline: She hasn't actually used them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Rachel moment - claims success while revealing she hasn't tried.

jokes.text: What? I love you.
jokes.setup: Joey's enthusiasm about his sweater vests
jokes.punchline: Chandler's overwhelmed emotional response
jokes.explanation: Chandler's emotional overwhelm at Joey's friendship, economically delivered.

jokes.text: So we don't have a real big budget. I figured I'd just stay in your room.
jokes.setup: Independent film with low budget
jokes.punchline: Joey assumes he can mooch off Chandler
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey mooching behavior, very character-appropriate.

jokes.text: For every dollar Shutterspeed makes, one penny of it goes right in Joey's pocket.
jokes.setup: Discussing Joey's movie payment
jokes.punchline: Terrible deal presented as good news
jokes.explanation: Joey's naivety about business deals, thinking 1% is generous.

jokes.text: Did you not hear the plot of the movie? 'She's been dead for 10 years.' I'm gonna be a millionaire.
jokes.setup: Chandler questions the movie's commercial viability
jokes.punchline: Joey thinks the terrible plot guarantees success
jokes.explanation: Great callback showing Joey's complete misunderstanding of what makes movies successful.

jokes.text: Oh, honey, I'll say goodbye to you at the car if you don't mind the pus.
jokes.setup: Chandler wants goodbye at the car
jokes.punchline: Monica's gross eye infection detail
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that's functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: But wait a minute. The pus is good. It means it's healing.
jokes.setup: Monica mentions pus
jokes.punchline: She tries to make it sound positive
jokes.explanation: Weak attempt to extend the pus joke without adding much.

jokes.text: Extended sequence of Phoebe ignoring Ross
jokes.setup: Ross tries to get Phoebe's attention
jokes.punchline: She completely ignores him reading
jokes.explanation: Extended awkward sequence showing Phoebe's passive-aggressive behavior.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry, I didn't see you there.
jokes.setup: Ross has been trying to get her attention
jokes.punchline: Obvious lie about not noticing him
jokes.explanation: Classic passive-aggressive response, well-executed for Phoebe.

jokes.text: Well, if you don't know, I can't help you. Well, I don't know. Well, I can't help you.
jokes.setup: Ross asks what he did wrong
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's circular logic trap
jokes.explanation: Perfect catch-22 that shows Phoebe's frustrating logic patterns.

jokes.text: Bye, fat ass.
jokes.setup: Seemingly friendly goodbye exchange
jokes.punchline: Sudden casual insult
jokes.explanation: Shocking casual cruelty that's both funny and disturbing.

jokes.text: But I am sorry about the 'fat ass' thing, because you actually have a very sweet little heinie.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's previous insult
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment that's almost worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - apology that's somehow more insulting than original insult.

jokes.text: We've been driving for a half-hour. And you haven't looked at the road once. Don't worry. It's out there.
jokes.setup: Chandler's concern about Joey's driving
jokes.punchline: Joey's casual dismissal of road safety
jokes.explanation: Great Joey logic - road exists therefore everything's fine.

jokes.text: Is this movie gonna be my big break? No. What? Yes. Dude, you said no.
jokes.setup: Rapid-fire question game
jokes.punchline: Chandler's honest answer destroys Joey's dreams
jokes.explanation: Devastating moment where the game format forces brutal honesty.

jokes.text: Wait. Wait. There's no sidewalk. Yeah. I'm gonna die here.
jokes.setup: Joey kicks Chandler out on a bridge
jokes.punchline: Chandler realizes the immediate danger
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the road trip conflict to literal danger.

jokes.text: You know, 'whom, whom.' Sometimes it's 'who.'
jokes.setup: Monica trying to figure out why Phoebe is mad at Ross
jokes.punchline: Ross's grammar corrections as annoying habit
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Ross's pedantic nature being genuinely annoying.

jokes.text: Okay, I'm the baby. Hey. Stop it.
jokes.setup: Monica suggests Ross acts like a baby when losing games
jokes.punchline: Ross immediately proves her point
jokes.explanation: Perfect demonstration of the character flaw being discussed.

jokes.text: All I said was that, uh, I didn't think this was gonna be his big break, that this movie wasn't gonna do anything for him, and that it didn't sound like a real movie. Okay, he should've pushed me off the bridge.
jokes.setup: Chandler explains what he said to Joey
jokes.punchline: Monica's assessment that he deserved worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where even Chandler realizes he was awful.

jokes.text: Who wants the teddy bear with one leg?
jokes.setup: Chandler brought roadside gifts to apologize
jokes.punchline: The pathetic nature of roadside garbage as gifts
jokes.explanation: Pathetic detail that shows how desperate Chandler's situation is.

jokes.text: My pillow's all wet. Well, you said it was practice.
jokes.setup: Monica tricks Rachel with 'practice' eye drops
jokes.punchline: Rachel's complaint reveals Monica used real drops
jokes.explanation: Monica's deception working, showing her determined controlling nature.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. You really are freakishly strong.
jokes.setup: Monica physically restraining Rachel for eye drops
jokes.punchline: Rachel's amazed comment about Monica's strength
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Monica's unexpected physical capabilities.

jokes.text: If Joey and Chandler walked in right now, we could make a fortune.
jokes.setup: Rachel and Monica in compromising wrestling position
jokes.punchline: Sexual misinterpretation opportunity
jokes.explanation: Cheap sexual humor that doesn't add much beyond surface-level innuendo.

jokes.text: Not as upset as he's gonna be when he finds out what I did with his sweater vests. Let's just say there's a well-dressed pack of dogs in Ohio.
jokes.setup: Joey's revenge on Chandler
jokes.punchline: Bizarre image of dogs wearing Chandler's clothing
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd revenge that's both vindictive and bizarrely charitable to dogs.

jokes.text: Oh, that's my grandma. And thanks, Joey. She's having a really great time.
jokes.setup: Joey finds mysterious box sliding around car
jokes.punchline: It's Phoebe's dead grandmother's ashes enjoying the trip
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe dark humor - treating dead grandmother like she's enjoying vacation.

jokes.text: I wonder where I could get a basket of porn? Oh, no, don't say I'm sorry with porn.
jokes.setup: Chandler wants to send Joey apology gift
jokes.punchline: Porn as apology gift, then rejection of idea
jokes.explanation: Absurd gift idea that's more shocking than clever.

jokes.text: You know what you should send him? A carton of cigarettes. Because that way he can trade it for protection. No. That's prison.
jokes.setup: Phoebe suggests cigarettes as gift
jokes.punchline: Monica realizes she's thinking of prison economy
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Phoebe confusing different institutional contexts.

jokes.text: Why are you mad at me? You said I was boring. Oh.
jokes.setup: Mystery of why Phoebe is mad
jokes.punchline: Simple, devastating reason revealed
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the mystery, though the reason is fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Remember that time on the frozen lake? We were playing. You said I was boring. And then you took off your energy mask and you were Cameron Diaz.
jokes.setup: Phoebe remembering when Ross called her boring
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd dream details
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Phoebe moment with wonderfully specific dream details.

jokes.text: Okay, there's a chance this may have been a dream.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's increasingly absurd 'memory'
jokes.punchline: Casual admission it might be a dream
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that deflates the absurd buildup beautifully.

jokes.text: It's probably just temporary. We're hoping to get more money soon, so if you could just, uh, hang out. Hang out? How long? I don't know. A week. Maybe two. The money'll turn up.
jokes.setup: Movie has been shut down due to money
jokes.punchline: Vague timeline and false optimism about funding
jokes.explanation: Realistic cringe humor about low-budget film industry incompetence.

jokes.text: Hey, you're not rich, are you? No. Worth a shot.
jokes.setup: Director's desperation for funding
jokes.punchline: Asking the talent for money as last resort
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of desperation, economically delivered.

jokes.text: Thanks a lot. Enjoy your stay at Caesar's. We hope it's toga-rific. Ugh. Kill me. Kill me now.
jokes.setup: Joey working at Caesar's Palace
jokes.punchline: Forced to say terrible pun, his humiliation
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing Joey's career disappointment through degrading service job.

jokes.text: Final eye drop wrestling sequence
jokes.setup: Monica's determination to give Rachel eye drops
jokes.punchline: Full physical restraint and forced medication
jokes.explanation: Escalated physical comedy that concludes the eye drop storyline.
